## Shared framing: climate projections, uncertainty, and interpretation {background-image="../../shared/assets/images/whale-shark-close.jpg" background-size="cover" background-position="center" .section-photo}

<div class="section-title-panel">
<h2>Shared framing</h2>
<p>How to read the models</p>
</div>

---

## Shared framing {background-color="white" .technical-white}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">How to read the models</h2>

<p class="framing-lead">Before using maps or SDM outputs, we need a shared way to interpret them.</p>

- Projections are “what-if” futures
- Uncertainty comes from the full workflow
- SDMs estimate suitability, not certainty
- Outputs are decision-support tools, not final answers

---

## Projection not prediction {background-color="white" .technical-white}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">Projection ≠ prediction</h2>

<div class="projection-layout">
<div class="definition-card">
<h3>Prediction</h3>
<p>“This is what will happen.”</p>
</div>
<div class="definition-card">
<h3>Projection</h3>
<p>“This is what could happen under these assumptions.”</p>

<div class="projection-figure">
<img src="../../shared/assets/images/tree-2023-projection-scenarios.png" alt="Future temperatures under different climate scenarios from Schoeman et al. 2023">
<p>Conceptual schematic adapted from Schoeman et al. (2023), <em>Trends in Ecology &amp; Evolution</em>. doi:<a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.tree.2023.04.005">10.1016/j.tree.2023.04.005</a></p>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<p class="key-message">For this workshop, climate projections help us explore plausible futures, not identify one exact future.</p>

---

## Where uncertainty enters {background-color="white" .technical-white}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">Where uncertainty enters</h2>

<p class="framing-lead">Uncertainty comes from the full chain.</p>

<svg class="uncertainty-figure" viewBox="0 0 1180 430" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Three sources of uncertainty across the modeling workflow.">
  <defs>
    <marker id="arrow-blue" markerWidth="9" markerHeight="8" refX="8" refY="4" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0,0 L9,4 L0,8 Z" fill="#1594d2"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="arrow-teal" markerWidth="9" markerHeight="8" refX="8" refY="4" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0,0 L9,4 L0,8 Z" fill="#007f8f"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="arrow-green" markerWidth="9" markerHeight="8" refX="8" refY="4" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0,0 L9,4 L0,8 Z" fill="#4d8f70"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>
  <g class="band climate">
    <rect class="band-bg" x="0" y="0" width="1180" height="112"/>
    <rect class="band-stripe" x="0" y="0" width="10" height="112"/>
    <text class="band-title" x="42" y="67">Climate assumptions</text>
    <path class="connector" d="M545 56 H725" marker-end="url(#arrow-blue)"/>
    <path class="connector" d="M820 56 H990" marker-end="url(#arrow-blue)"/>
    <g class="node" transform="translate(430 26)"><rect width="150" height="60" rx="30"/><text x="75" y="38">Scenario</text></g>
    <g class="node" transform="translate(705 26)"><rect width="190" height="60" rx="30"/><text x="95" y="38">Climate model</text></g>
    <g class="node" transform="translate(975 26)"><rect width="150" height="60" rx="30"/><text x="75" y="38">Model run</text></g>
  </g>
  <g class="band regional" transform="translate(0 142)">
    <rect class="band-bg" x="0" y="0" width="1180" height="112"/>
    <rect class="band-stripe" x="0" y="0" width="10" height="112"/>
    <text class="band-title" x="42" y="67">Regional translation</text>
    <path class="connector" d="M575 56 H760" marker-end="url(#arrow-teal)"/>
    <path class="connector" d="M850 56 H990" marker-end="url(#arrow-teal)"/>
    <g class="node" transform="translate(430 26)"><rect width="190" height="60" rx="30"/><text x="95" y="38">Downscaling</text></g>
    <g class="node" transform="translate(745 26)"><rect width="145" height="60" rx="30"/><text x="72" y="38">Depth layer</text></g>
    <g class="node wide" transform="translate(965 26)"><rect width="185" height="60" rx="30"/><text x="92" y="38">Variable choice</text></g>
  </g>
  <g class="band biological" transform="translate(0 284)">
    <rect class="band-bg" x="0" y="0" width="1180" height="112"/>
    <rect class="band-stripe" x="0" y="0" width="10" height="112"/>
    <text class="band-title" x="42" y="67">Biological interpretation</text>
    <path class="connector" d="M535 56 H700" marker-end="url(#arrow-green)"/>
    <path class="connector" d="M820 56 H970" marker-end="url(#arrow-green)"/>
    <g class="node" transform="translate(430 26)"><rect width="135" height="60" rx="30"/><text x="68" y="38">Records</text></g>
    <g class="node" transform="translate(690 26)"><rect width="170" height="60" rx="30"/><text x="85" y="38">SDM algorithm</text></g>
    <g class="node wide" transform="translate(950 26)"><rect width="190" height="60" rx="30"/><text x="95" y="38">Conservation use</text></g>
  </g>
</svg>

<p class="key-message">Uncertainty is not failure; it tells us where to be cautious.</p>

---

## Climate-side uncertainty {background-color="white" .technical-white}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">Climate-side uncertainty</h2>

- Scenario: which future climate pathway?
- Model: which global climate model?
- Run: which realization / initial conditions?
- Downscaling: how do we move from coarse to regional?
- Depth / variables: surface, mid-water, seafloor, oxygen, temperature, currents, etc.

---

## Biological and SDM uncertainty {background-color="white" .technical-white}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">Biological and SDM uncertainty</h2>

- Records: where do we have data, and where are gaps?
- Sampling bias: absent, or just not sampled?
- Predictors: which variables define the niche?
- Algorithm: which statistical approach?
- Suitability: suitable does not always mean present, abundant, or critical habitat.

---

## What SDMs show {background-color="white" .technical-white}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">SDMs estimate environmental suitability</h2>

They do not directly show:

<div class="not-list">

- confirmed presence
- abundance
- reproduction
- population trends
- management priority by themselves

</div>

<p class="key-message">Suitable ≠ occupied ≠ important habitat</p>

---

## How we use the outputs {background-color="white" .technical-white}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">Use model outputs as decision-support</h2>

They can help identify:

- possible climate exposure
- future suitable areas
- data gaps
- areas needing monitoring
- places where planning may need to be adaptive

<p class="key-message">Interpret outputs with expert knowledge, local context, and governance feasibility.</p>

---

## Workshop rule {background-color="white" .technical-white}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">The goal is not to find “the perfect map”</h2>

The goal is to ask:

- What patterns are consistent?
- What looks uncertain?
- What needs expert review?
- What can improve in the next version?
